timeout.description: number of seconds the request was given

datasource.description: The server name you would like data from
datasource.name: datasource

language.description: Language to use in the response, takes precedence over Accept-Language
language.name: language

page.description: Which page of results to return
page.name: page

token.description: Access token to use if unable to set a header
token.name: token

blueprint_location_id.title: get_characters_character_id_industry_jobs_blueprint_location_id

completed_character_id.description: ID of the character which completed this job

completed_date.description: Date and time when this job was completed

cost.description: The sume of job installation fee and industry facility tax
cost.title: get_characters_character_id_industry_jobs_cost

end_date.description: Date and time when this job finished
end_date.title: get_characters_character_id_industry_jobs_end_date

facility_id.description: ID of the facility where this job is running

output_location_id.title: get_characters_character_id_industry_jobs_output_location_id

pause_date.description: Date and time when this job was paused (i.e. time when the facility where this job was installed went offline)

product_type_id.description: Type ID of product (manufactured, copied or invented)

runs.description: Number of runs for a manufacturing job, or number of copies to make for a blueprint copy

start_date.description: Date and time when this job started

station_id.description: ID of the station where industry facility is located
station_id.title: get_characters_character_id_industry_jobs_station_id

successful_runs.description: Number of successful runs for this job. Equal to runs unless this is an invention job

get.description:
Returns a character's wallet balance

Alternate route: `/v1/characters/{character_id}/wallet/`

---
This route is cached for up to 120 seconds

---
[Diff of the upcoming changes](https://esi.evetech.net/diff/latest/dev/#GET-/characters/{character_id}/wallet/)

Retrieve the given character's wallet journal going 30 days back

---
Alternate route: `/dev/characters/{character_id}/wallet/journal/`

Alternate route: `/legacy/characters/{character_id}/wallet/journal/`

Alternate route: `/v5/characters/{character_id}/wallet/journal/`

Alternate route: `/v6/characters/{character_id}/wallet/journal/`

---
This route is cached for up to 3600 seconds

amount.description: The amount of ISK given or taken from the wallet as a result of the given transaction. Positive when ISK is deposited into the wallet and negative when ISK is withdrawn
amount.title: get_characters_character_id_wallet_journal_amount

context_id.description: An ID that gives extra context to the particular transaction. Because of legacy reasons the context is completely different per ref_type and means different things. It is also possible to not have a context_id
context_id.title: get_characters_character_id_wallet_journal_context_id

context_id_type.description: The type of the given context_id if present
context_id_type.title: get_characters_character_id_wallet_journal_context_id_type

description.description: The reason for the transaction, mirrors what is seen in the client
description.title: get_characters_character_id_wallet_journal_description

first_party_id.description: The id of the first party involved in the transaction. This attribute has no consistency and is different or non existant for particular ref_types. The description attribute will help make sense of what this attribute means. For more info about the given ID it can be dropped into the /universe/names/ ESI route to determine its type and name
first_party_id.title: get_characters_character_id_wallet_journal_first_party_id

reason.description: The user stated reason for the transaction. Only applies to some ref_types
reason.title: get_characters_character_id_wallet_journal_reason

ref_type.description: "The transaction type for the given. transaction. Different transaction types will populate different attributes."
ref_type.title: get_characters_character_id_wallet_journal_ref_type

second_party_id.description: The id of the second party involved in the transaction. This attribute has no consistency and is different or non existant for particular ref_types. The description attribute will help make sense of what this attribute means. For more info about the given ID it can be dropped into the /universe/names/ ESI route to determine its type and name
second_party_id.title: get_characters_character_id_wallet_journal_second_party_id

tax.description: Tax amount received. Only applies to tax related transactions
tax.title: get_characters_character_id_wallet_journal_tax

tax_receiver_id.description: The corporation ID receiving any tax paid. Only applies to tax related transactions
tax_receiver_id.title: get_characters_character_id_wallet_journal_tax_receiver_id

get.description:
Retrieve the given corporation's wallet journal for the given division going 30 days back

---
Alternate route: `/dev/corporations/{corporation_id}/wallets/{division}/journal/`

Alternate route: `/legacy/corporations/{corporation_id}/wallets/{division}/journal/`

Alternate route: `/v3/corporations/{corporation_id}/wallets/{division}/journal/`

Alternate route: `/v4/corporations/{corporation_id}/wallets/{division}/journal/`

---
This route is cached for up to 3600 seconds

---
Requires one of the following EVE corporation role(s): Accountant, Junior_Accountant

parameters.description: Wallet key of the division to fetch journals from
parameters.name: division

application/json.description: CCP Zoetrope transferred cash from C C P's corporate account to CCP SnowedIn's account

amount.description: The amount of ISK given or taken from the wallet as a result of the given transaction. Positive when ISK is deposited into the wallet and negative when ISK is withdrawn
amount.title: get_corporations_corporation_id_wallets_division_journal_amount

context_id.description: An ID that gives extra context to the particular transaction. Because of legacy reasons the context is completely different per ref_type and means different things. It is also possible to not have a context_id
context_id.title: get_corporations_corporation_id_wallets_division_journal_context_id

context_id_type.description: The type of the given context_id if present
context_id_type.title: get_corporations_corporation_id_wallets_division_journal_context_id_type

description.description: The reason for the transaction, mirrors what is seen in the client
description.title: get_corporations_corporation_id_wallets_division_journal_description

first_party_id.description: The id of the first party involved in the transaction. This attribute has no consistency and is different or non existant for particular ref_types. The description attribute will help make sense of what this attribute means. For more info about the given ID it can be dropped into the /universe/names/ ESI route to determine its type and name
first_party_id.title: get_corporations_corporation_id_wallets_division_journal_first_party_id

reason.description: The user stated reason for the transaction. Only applies to some ref_types
reason.title: get_corporations_corporation_id_wallets_division_journal_reason

ref_type.description: "The transaction type for the given. transaction. Different transaction types will populate different attributes. Note: If you have an existing XML API application that is using ref_types, you will need to know which string ESI ref_type maps to which integer. You can look at the following file to see string->int mappings: https://github.com/ccpgames/eve-glue/blob/master/eve_glue/wallet_journal_ref.py"
ref_type.title: get_corporations_corporation_id_wallets_division_journal_ref_type

second_party_id.description: The id of the second party involved in the transaction. This attribute has no consistency and is different or non existant for particular ref_types. The description attribute will help make sense of what this attribute means. For more info about the given ID it can be dropped into the /universe/names/ ESI route to determine its type and name
second_party_id.title: get_corporations_corporation_id_wallets_division_journal_second_party_id

tax.description: Tax amount received. Only applies to tax related transactions
tax.title: get_corporations_corporation_id_wallets_division_journal_tax

tax_receiver_id.description: The corporation ID receiving any tax paid. Only applies to tax related transactions
tax_receiver_id.title: get_corporations_corporation_id_wallets_division_journal_tax_receiver_id

404.description: Ensure all IDs are valid before resolving

post.summary: Get names and categories for a set of IDs
